title: Subculture & Diaspora Archive: Community Self-Documentation Platform
verdict: CONDITIONAL BUILD - Requires significant pivots to organizational partnership model, nonprofit structure, and integration with existing tools. Not viable as venture-backed standalone platform. Viable as nonprofit infrastructure embedded in trusted organizations. Timing window is 2-5 years before AI companies absorb archival layer. Expertise gap is real and requires deep team composition (community organizer, security engineer, archivist, data governance expert, lawyer). LLM capability fit is weak—this requires domain expertise that cannot be substituted with AI.

title: Hearing Aid Device-as-Service with BNPL Financing
verdict: BUILD with mandatory pivots: (1) Outcome-based pricing with success metrics, (2) Integrated audiological support, (3) B2B2C partnerships with retailers, (4) Lean team with domain expert advisors. The core idea survives, but execution must address structural adoption failure through risk management, not just financing.

verdict: BUILD with mandatory mutations: (1) Reposition from generalist to domain-specific (biotech); (2) Treat SIGIL as a component of xBrain, not a standalone product; (3) Use only official APIs, no scraping; (4) Hire biotech domain expert; (5) Prove downstream ROI within 3 months. The original idea (generalist signal aggregation) is a commodity and will fail. The mutated idea (domain-specific signal layer for biotech idea generation) is viable if executed with domain expertise and speed-to-market focus.
